title: buildah bud --network=none should have no network
body:
Fixes: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/4227

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: nice catch @Luap99 !  And a great example of why @edsantiago reminds us frequently to not only test the return status, but also the value we get back from a call.

and it looks like the new tests isn't failing as expected.  No failure is being raised.

```
[+1730s] not ok 373 build-with-network-test

[+1730s] #  from function `run_buildah' in file ./helpers.bash, line 301,
[+1730s] #  in test file ./bud.bats, line 5236)
.
.
.
[+1730s] # $ /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests/./../bin/buildah build --network=none --signature-policy /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests/./policy.json /var/tmp/buildah_tests.tqdeml
[+1730s] # STEP 1/2: FROM alpine
```

@Luap99 The other tests is on the success path.  I plan a follow update to the newly added tests. but it is much more involved in that it requires changes to imagebuilder.
@TomSweeneyRedHat the prior tests was not attempting to do networking, so it is successful.

> There is already a network=none test,
> 
> https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/f0b2d39b83cd3bae8a6d0ef8c05fd384f81412a0/tests/bud.bats#L3832
> 
> Please fix the test instead of adding more.

@Luap99 upon checking this, the suggested test is just checking sanity of option of not actually testing the network, I will refine all tests for network with implementation of https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/4230 in a different PR

I am not sure if this containerized integration is running on `cgroupv1` or `cgroupv2` but the mounted `/sys/fs/cgroup` must be writable for `--isolation oci` to work and I don't think this will be allowed on `cgroupv1` since delegation does not works on `cgroupv1` and it must be `ro` inside the container.

But I was able to make similar thing work with with `cgroupv2` on my local with extra `--privileged`, it looks like `--security-opt=label=disable --security-opt=seccomp=unconfined --cap-add=all` is not as equal to `--privileged` so I think this may need following diff but i have only tested on `v2` and started first container manually using `podman` not the cirrus script.

```diff
diff --git a/contrib/cirrus/lib.sh b/contrib/cirrus/lib.sh
index 31278ec8..7d1f61fa 100755
--- a/contrib/cirrus/lib.sh
+++ b/contrib/cirrus/lib.sh
@@ -184,6 +184,7 @@ in_podman() {
                    --security-opt=label=disable \
                    --security-opt=seccomp=unconfined \
                    --cap-add=all \
+                   --privileged \
                    --env-file=$envfile \
                    -e "GOPATH=$GOPATH" \
                    -e "GOSRC=$GOSRC" \
```

@rhatdan @giuseppe overall i see lot of direct cgroup tests are being skipped when running inside container so not sure if `--isolation oci` will work on in this CI maybe as last option adding `skip_if_in_container` to skip this test in container could be an option ?

The issue which as failing, was that buildah automatically runs within a container with --isolation=chroot and --network option has no effect in this situation.  I opened an issue with buildah to throw an error when running --isolation=chroot and --network *

https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4250
